lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The coffee machine runs a cycle nobody ordered. Theo stands at the open locker, the complaint on two pages across his palm.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He reads them as he has memorized them — not for the words but for the gaps. The names. The dates. The documented diversions. Three in six weeks. His signature not yet at the bottom.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The door.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He folds the pages. The locker shuts before she is fully inside.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: I left my lanyard.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: It's on the table.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She crosses the room. Picks it up. Doesn't look at him. He leans against the locker with arms folded — the posture of someone who arrived already like this.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Long shift.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: Still four hours.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She loops the lanyard around her neck. The coffee machine finishes whatever it started. She could leave now. He could say something that matters. The room holds both of them with the careful neutrality of spaces that have seen worse.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: The cardiac transfer on four. Did you hear?

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: I heard he was stable.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: He was. For a while.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: What does that mean?

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She looks at him for the first time since she came in.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: It means stable for a while. That's what it means.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He nods. The locker cold against his spine. Two pages folded in thirds inside it. Arms still crossed.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: You look tired.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: Everybody looks tired at three in the morning.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Not like that.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: A pause. Long enough to be a choice.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: I'm fine, Maeve.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She doesn't argue with this, which means she doesn't believe it. She turns her lanyard over in one hand. There is something she came in for that wasn't the lanyard. Both of them know this.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: I pulled the chart on the Morrow woman. Before the diversion. The documentation was—

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: Maeve.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: It was incomplete.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She stops. That lands somewhere. He watches her register it — that he already knows, that there are things Theo has carried, privately, for longer than she realized.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: How long have you—

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: A while.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The coffee machine makes a sound. Nothing pours.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: And you haven't—

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: This is the conversation they are having. The one running beneath the one they appear to be having. Maeve puts her hand flat on the table without leaning — just the gesture of someone who needs a moment that a surface can provide.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: There's a board review next quarter.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: If someone were to—

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: I know, Maeve.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She lifts her hand. Looks at the door. He looks at the locker.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: I'm not asking you to do anything.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: I know that too.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She nods. The only agreement they reach. She moves toward the door and pauses with her hand on the frame but doesn't look back.

lines.speaker: Dr. Maeve Callahan
lines.text: The cardiac transfer on four. His name is Osei.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She leaves. The door pulls shut with the soft authority of hospital doors — designed to close without noise, which they always do, even when the closing is very loud.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo stays where he stands. Arms still crossed. The locker at his back.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: After a moment he turns and opens it. Unfolds the pages. Reads the first line.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Folds them again. Same thirds. Same crease.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The lanyard she left with will keep a name badge centered all shift. The name on his complaint has no badge. No corridor. No shift.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He closes the locker.
